findingDetails.title: Completed research phases retain pre-run continuity state
findingDetails.evidence: Parent continuity remains 0% and directs phase 001 execution while the phase map marks 001-003 complete; phase-002/003 spec continuity remains 10% and directs already-completed research dispatch while their body status and implementation summaries report complete/100%.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact continuity/status inventory across the parent and all ten configured child packets found stale completed-phase continuity in the parent and phase-002/003 specs; planned phases 004-010 consistently remain 0% with unchecked checklists.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reconcile the stale continuity blocks through the owning continuity workflow and refresh derived metadata.

claimAdjudication.claim: Canonical packet surfaces give obsolete next actions and completion percentages for already-completed research phases.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Checked later status tables, implementation-summary frontmatter, parent phase map, all planned child lifecycle fields, and phase-parent graph metadata.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Child resume normally prefers implementation-summary continuity and phase-parent resume may list children, containing some runtime impact but not removing contradictory canonical metadata.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 only if the completion validator and every memory/resume consumer are proven to exclude these spec.md continuity fields.

traceabilityChecks.result: Current sk-design runtime lacks planned packet-011 retrieval/proof fields, consistent with phases 004-010 remaining planned.
Planned phases 004-010 have no checked completion evidence; completed-phase stale continuity recorded as P1.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Spec-folder correctness inventory requires lifecycle matrix and current-contract reconciliation.
